images.caption: Figure 1:Given a coarse visual sample (left one in each pair) as the guidance, our method generates its corresponding refined result (right one) in a training-free manner.

Figure 2:Existing and our solutions.(a) Training translation networks based on paired data, which is costly and non-generalizable to different types of coarse samples. (b) Solving inverse problems based on a known forward operator, making it is not robust. (c) Adding noise to the coarse sample and denoising it, which is difficult to balance the guidance and quality. (d) Our method leverages thehh-transform to achieve training-free, operator-free, and stable coarse-guided generation.

Figure 3:Overview of Weightedhh-Transform Sampling. (a) If we have𝒉𝒙0=𝒚{\color[rgb]{0.75390625,0,0}\definecolor[named]{pgfstrokecolor}{rgb}{0.75390625,0,0}\bm{h}_{\bm{x}_{0}=\bm{y}}}, the generation result will be the ideal sample. (b) We leverage𝒉𝒙0=𝒚~{\color[rgb]{0,0.69140625,0.3125}\definecolor[named]{pgfstrokecolor}{rgb}{0,0.69140625,0.3125}\bm{h}_{\bm{x}_{0}=\widetilde{\bm{y}}}}to approximate the untractable𝒉𝒙0=𝒚{\color[rgb]{0.75390625,0,0}\definecolor[named]{pgfstrokecolor}{rgb}{0.75390625,0,0}\bm{h}_{\bm{x}_{0}=\bm{y}}}and derive that the error is increasing gradually during the sampling process. (c) To mitigate the error influence, we decrease the approximation weight and finally generate a high-quality refined sample.

Figure 4:Qualitative results of coarse-guided image generation. Compared with training-free SDEdit, our method shows more faithful synthesis across tasks. For fairness, we take their own commonly-used hyper-parameter for SDEdit (t0=500t_{0}=500) and ours (α=5\alpha=5), and all other settings are the same.
Figure 5:Qualitative comparisons on the subset of DL3DV-10K.Our method shows better appearance alignment to the ground truth (see highlighted blue boxes).
Figure 7:Super-resolution results by using differentα\alpha. A largeα\alphaleads to a small weight, and vice versa (weight schedulerλσ=σtα\lambda_{\sigma}=\sigma_{t}^{\alpha}).
Figure 8:Our method is compatible with both score-based CogVideoX and Flow-based Wan2.2.
Figure A9:Additional qualitative comparisons with SDEdit on image restoration tasks.
Figure A10:Additional camera-controlled video generation qualitative comparisons.
Figure A11:Additional camera-controlled video generation results using Wan2.2.
Figure A12:Text-based image editing comparisons with three baselines on PIE-Bench
